title: rubus rivularis subsp. leptobelus
intro: Rubus rivularis subsp. leptobelus, also known as the mountain raspberry, is a lesser-known sub-species of the Rubus rivularis plant, a type of bramble native to the mountainous regions of Japan. The mountain raspberry has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus rivularis subsp. leptobelus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antidiabetic agent. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
